short: NVIDIA's library for compiling LLMs into highly optimized GPU inference engines.
definition: TensorRT-LLM (often abbreviated TRT-LLM) is NVIDIA's open-source library for optimizing and running LLM inference on NVIDIA GPUs. It builds on TensorRT to compile a model into a hardware-specific engine, applying kernel fusion, quantization (FP8/FP4/INT8), in-flight batching, and paged KV cache. The result is very low latency and high throughput on NVIDIA hardware, at the cost of an ahead-of-time build step. It is one of the engines underneath NVIDIA NIM and Triton Inference Server.
